findings.evidence: Lines 70-72 define `canonicalCharacteristic := P.weight`, and lines 99-103 prove it equals the basis self-pairing; for the standard plumbing canonical class, a `-2` vertex has canonical coordinate `0`, while this API returns `-2`.
findings.fix: Change `canonicalCharacteristic` to `fun v => -P.weight v - 2` and update/remove the dependent theorem equating it with `intersectionForm (Pi.single v 1) (Pi.single v 1)`; if `P.weight` is only intended as a parity basepoint, rename it instead of calling it canonical.
findings.issue: `canonicalCharacteristic` is defined as `P.weight`, but the canonical characteristic class for a sphere plumbing satisfies the adjunction convention `K(E_v) + E_v·E_v = -2`, hence coordinates `-P.weight v - 2`, not the self-pairing itself.

summary: The parity predicate is faithful, but the PR misidentifies the standard canonical characteristic covector used in plumbing/lattice homology.
